entries.title: Here's a hack you wish you knew sooner: Find wall studs with a magnet
entries.hook: Here's a hack you wish you knew sooner:
hacks: Find wall studs with a magnet
explanations: Run a magnet along the wall until it sticks to a screw or nail. That's where the stud is.
Place a rubber band between the screwdriver and the screw head. The extra grip lets you turn it out.
Fold cables in thirds and loop a twist tie around the middle. They stay coiled and never tangle in your bag.
Dip a toothpick in wood glue and hammer it into the loose joint. Snap off the excess. The table becomes rock solid.

entries.title: Here's a life hack that actually works: Find wall studs with a magnet

hacks: Find wall studs with a magnet
explanations: Run a magnet along the wall until it sticks to a screw or nail. That's where the stud is.
Fold the top of the bag down in triangles then flip it over. The tension holds it shut without a clip.
Heat the sticker with a hairdryer for 30 seconds. The adhesive softens and it peels off without residue.
Store ice cream in a ziplock bag inside the carton. The bag prevents ice crystals from forming so it stays scoopable.

entries.title: This simple trick changes everything: Keep bananas fresh longer
entries.hook: This simple trick changes everything:
explanations: Wrap the stem of the banana bunch in plastic wrap. It traps the ethylene gas and slows ripening by days.
Wrap a spring from an old pen around the base of the charging cable. It prevents fraying at the stress point.
Chew gum while chopping onions. The chewing forces you to breathe through your mouth bypassing eye-irritating fumes.
Dip a toothpick in wood glue and hammer it into the loose joint. Snap off the excess. The table becomes rock solid.

entries.title: This simple trick changes everything: Keep chip bags closed
entries.hook: This simple trick changes everything:
explanations: Fold the top of the bag down in triangles then flip it over. The tension holds it shut without a clip.
Draw over the stain with a dry erase marker then wipe both off together. The solvents lift the permanent ink.
Place a curved piece of aluminum foil behind your router. It reflects the signal forward and can double range in one direction.
Store ice cream in a ziplock bag inside the carton. The bag prevents ice crystals from forming so it stays scoopable.

entries.title: Here's a hack you wish you knew sooner: Peel garlic in 10 seconds
entries.hook: Here's a hack you wish you knew sooner:
explanations: Put garlic cloves in a metal bowl cover with another bowl and shake hard for 10 seconds. The skin falls right off.
Stick a fork into the knot and twist. The tines separate the strands and the knot loosens instantly.
Wrap duct tape around the lid in opposite directions leaving two tails to pull. The leverage opens any jar easily.
Place a curved piece of aluminum foil behind your router. It reflects the signal forward and can double range in one direction.

entries.title: Here's a life hack that actually works: Cool drinks faster
entries.hook: Here's a life hack that actually works:
explanations: Wrap a wet paper towel around the bottle and put it in the freezer. Evaporative cooling works in 15 minutes.
Chew gum while chopping onions. The chewing forces you to breathe through your mouth bypassing eye-irritating fumes.
Wrap duct tape around the lid in opposite directions leaving two tails to pull. The leverage opens any jar easily.
Rub pencil graphite along the teeth of a stuck zipper. The graphite acts as dry lubricant and it glides smoothly.

entries.title: This simple trick changes everything: Make phone charger last
entries.hook: This simple trick changes everything:
explanations: Wrap a spring from an old pen around the base of the charging cable. It prevents fraying at the stress point.
Draw over the stain with a dry erase marker then wipe both off together. The solvents lift the permanent ink.
Store wireless earbuds in the case upside down. Contacts don't connect so trickle charging stops and battery lasts longer.
Fold cables in thirds and loop a twist tie around the middle. They stay coiled and never tangle in your bag.

entries.title: Here's a life hack that actually works: Peel garlic in 10 seconds
entries.hook: Here's a life hack that actually works:
hacks: Find wall studs with a magnet
explanations: Put garlic cloves in a metal bowl cover with another bowl and shake hard for 10 seconds. The skin falls right off.
Run a magnet along the wall until it sticks to a screw or nail. That's where the stud is.
Fold the top of the bag down in triangles then flip it over. The tension holds it shut without a clip.
Wrap a wet paper towel around the bottle and put it in the freezer. Evaporative cooling works in 15 minutes.

entries.title: This simple trick changes everything: Never lose a sock again
entries.hook: This simple trick changes everything:
explanations: Safety pin socks together before washing. They stay paired through the entire laundry cycle.
Place a rubber band between the screwdriver and the screw head. The extra grip lets you turn it out.
Wrap duct tape around the lid in opposite directions leaving two tails to pull. The leverage opens any jar easily.
Store wireless earbuds in the case upside down. Contacts don't connect so trickle charging stops and battery lasts longer.

entries.title: You've been doing this wrong your whole life: Untie knots with a fork
entries.hook: You've been doing this wrong your whole life:
hacks: Find wall studs with a magnet
explanations: Stick a fork into the knot and twist. The tines separate the strands and the knot loosens instantly.
Run a magnet along the wall until it sticks to a screw or nail. That's where the stud is.
Rub pencil graphite along the teeth of a stuck zipper. The graphite acts as dry lubricant and it glides smoothly.
Dip a toothpick in wood glue and hammer it into the loose joint. Snap off the excess. The table becomes rock solid.

entries.title: Here's a life hack that actually works: Untie knots with a fork
entries.hook: Here's a life hack that actually works:
explanations: Stick a fork into the knot and twist. The tines separate the strands and the knot loosens instantly.
Place a rubber band between the screwdriver and the screw head. The extra grip lets you turn it out.
Chew gum while chopping onions. The chewing forces you to breathe through your mouth bypassing eye-irritating fumes.
Heat the sticker with a hairdryer for 30 seconds. The adhesive softens and it peels off without residue.

entries.hook: You've been doing this wrong your whole life:
explanations: Wrap the stem of the banana bunch in plastic wrap. It traps the ethylene gas and slows ripening by days.

Wrap duct tape around the lid in opposite directions leaving two tails to pull. The leverage opens any jar easily.

entries.title: This simple trick changes everything: Remove a stripped screw
entries.hook: This simple trick changes everything:
explanations: Place a rubber band between the screwdriver and the screw head. The extra grip lets you turn it out.
Draw over the stain with a dry erase marker then wipe both off together. The solvents lift the permanent ink.
Wrap duct tape around the lid in opposite directions leaving two tails to pull. The leverage opens any jar easily.
Rub pencil graphite along the teeth of a stuck zipper. The graphite acts as dry lubricant and it glides smoothly.

entries.title: This life hack will save you time: Keep bananas fresh longer
entries.hook: This life hack will save you time:
explanations: Wrap the stem of the banana bunch in plastic wrap. It traps the ethylene gas and slows ripening by days.
Chew gum while chopping onions. The chewing forces you to breathe through your mouth bypassing eye-irritating fumes.
Dip a toothpick in wood glue and hammer it into the loose joint. Snap off the excess. The table becomes rock solid.
Microwave the lemon for 15 seconds before squeezing. Heat breaks down membranes and releases twice the juice.

entries.title: Here's a hack you wish you knew sooner: Make phone charger last
entries.hook: Here's a hack you wish you knew sooner:
explanations: Wrap a spring from an old pen around the base of the charging cable. It prevents fraying at the stress point.
Place a rubber band between the screwdriver and the screw head. The extra grip lets you turn it out.
Fold cables in thirds and loop a twist tie around the middle. They stay coiled and never tangle in your bag.
Store ice cream in a ziplock bag inside the carton. The bag prevents ice crystals from forming so it stays scoopable.

entries.title: You've been doing this wrong your whole life: Double headphone battery

explanations: Store wireless earbuds in the case upside down. Contacts don't connect so trickle charging stops and battery lasts longer.
Rub pencil graphite along the teeth of a stuck zipper. The graphite acts as dry lubricant and it glides smoothly.
Heat the sticker with a hairdryer for 30 seconds. The adhesive softens and it peels off without residue.
Dip a toothpick in wood glue and hammer it into the loose joint. Snap off the excess. The table becomes rock solid.

Run a magnet along the wall until it sticks to a screw or nail. That's where the stud is.
Fold the top of the bag down in triangles then flip it over. The tension holds it shut without a clip.
Store wireless earbuds in the case upside down. Contacts don't connect so trickle charging stops and battery lasts longer.

entries.title: Here's a life hack that actually works: Find wall studs with a magnet
entries.hook: Here's a life hack that actually works:
hacks: Find wall studs with a magnet
explanations: Run a magnet along the wall until it sticks to a screw or nail. That's where the stud is.
Wrap duct tape around the lid in opposite directions leaving two tails to pull. The leverage opens any jar easily.
Rub pencil graphite along the teeth of a stuck zipper. The graphite acts as dry lubricant and it glides smoothly.
Microwave the lemon for 15 seconds before squeezing. Heat breaks down membranes and releases twice the juice.

entries.title: You've been doing this wrong your whole life: Prevent onion tears
entries.hook: You've been doing this wrong your whole life:
explanations: Chew gum while chopping onions. The chewing forces you to breathe through your mouth bypassing eye-irritating fumes.
Store wireless earbuds in the case upside down. Contacts don't connect so trickle charging stops and battery lasts longer.
Place a curved piece of aluminum foil behind your router. It reflects the signal forward and can double range in one direction.
Microwave the lemon for 15 seconds before squeezing. Heat breaks down membranes and releases twice the juice.

entries.title: This life hack will save you time: Keep bananas fresh longer

explanations: Wrap the stem of the banana bunch in plastic wrap. It traps the ethylene gas and slows ripening by days.
Run a magnet along the wall until it sticks to a screw or nail. That's where the stud is.
Draw over the stain with a dry erase marker then wipe both off together. The solvents lift the permanent ink.
Store ice cream in a ziplock bag inside the carton. The bag prevents ice crystals from forming so it stays scoopable.

explanations: Draw over the stain with a dry erase marker then wipe both off together. The solvents lift the permanent ink.
Store wireless earbuds in the case upside down. Contacts don't connect so trickle charging stops and battery lasts longer.
Heat the sticker with a hairdryer for 30 seconds. The adhesive softens and it peels off without residue.
Microwave the lemon for 15 seconds before squeezing. Heat breaks down membranes and releases twice the juice.

entries.title: Here's a life hack that actually works: Keep bananas fresh longer
entries.hook: Here's a life hack that actually works:
explanations: Wrap the stem of the banana bunch in plastic wrap. It traps the ethylene gas and slows ripening by days.
Draw over the stain with a dry erase marker then wipe both off together. The solvents lift the permanent ink.
Chew gum while chopping onions. The chewing forces you to breathe through your mouth bypassing eye-irritating fumes.
Rub pencil graphite along the teeth of a stuck zipper. The graphite acts as dry lubricant and it glides smoothly.

entries.title: This life hack will save you time: Peel garlic in 10 seconds
entries.hook: This life hack will save you time:
explanations: Put garlic cloves in a metal bowl cover with another bowl and shake hard for 10 seconds. The skin falls right off.
Wrap a spring from an old pen around the base of the charging cable. It prevents fraying at the stress point.
Draw over the stain with a dry erase marker then wipe both off together. The solvents lift the permanent ink.
Fold cables in thirds and loop a twist tie around the middle. They stay coiled and never tangle in your bag.

entries.title: Here's a life hack that actually works: Never lose a sock again
entries.hook: Here's a life hack that actually works:
explanations: Safety pin socks together before washing. They stay paired through the entire laundry cycle.
Wrap duct tape around the lid in opposite directions leaving two tails to pull. The leverage opens any jar easily.
Place a curved piece of aluminum foil behind your router. It reflects the signal forward and can double range in one direction.
Dip a toothpick in wood glue and hammer it into the loose joint. Snap off the excess. The table becomes rock solid.

explanations: Draw over the stain with a dry erase marker then wipe both off together. The solvents lift the permanent ink.
Store wireless earbuds in the case upside down. Contacts don't connect so trickle charging stops and battery lasts longer.
Rub pencil graphite along the teeth of a stuck zipper. The graphite acts as dry lubricant and it glides smoothly.
Microwave the lemon for 15 seconds before squeezing. Heat breaks down membranes and releases twice the juice.

Chew gum while chopping onions. The chewing forces you to breathe through your mouth bypassing eye-irritating fumes.
Wrap duct tape around the lid in opposite directions leaving two tails to pull. The leverage opens any jar easily.
Store wireless earbuds in the case upside down. Contacts don't connect so trickle charging stops and battery lasts longer.

entries.title: Here's a hack you wish you knew sooner: Keep bananas fresh longer
entries.hook: Here's a hack you wish you knew sooner:
explanations: Wrap the stem of the banana bunch in plastic wrap. It traps the ethylene gas and slows ripening by days.
Draw over the stain with a dry erase marker then wipe both off together. The solvents lift the permanent ink.
Chew gum while chopping onions. The chewing forces you to breathe through your mouth bypassing eye-irritating fumes.
Microwave the lemon for 15 seconds before squeezing. Heat breaks down membranes and releases twice the juice.

entries.title: This life hack will save you time: Peel garlic in 10 seconds
entries.hook: This life hack will save you time:
explanations: Put garlic cloves in a metal bowl cover with another bowl and shake hard for 10 seconds. The skin falls right off.
Wrap the stem of the banana bunch in plastic wrap. It traps the ethylene gas and slows ripening by days.
Store wireless earbuds in the case upside down. Contacts don't connect so trickle charging stops and battery lasts longer.
Fold cables in thirds and loop a twist tie around the middle. They stay coiled and never tangle in your bag.

Rub pencil graphite along the teeth of a stuck zipper. The graphite acts as dry lubricant and it glides smoothly.
Fold cables in thirds and loop a twist tie around the middle. They stay coiled and never tangle in your bag.
Heat the sticker with a hairdryer for 30 seconds. The adhesive softens and it peels off without residue.

entries.title: Here's a hack you wish you knew sooner: Peel garlic in 10 seconds
entries.hook: Here's a hack you wish you knew sooner:
explanations: Put garlic cloves in a metal bowl cover with another bowl and shake hard for 10 seconds. The skin falls right off.
Wrap duct tape around the lid in opposite directions leaving two tails to pull. The leverage opens any jar easily.
Place a curved piece of aluminum foil behind your router. It reflects the signal forward and can double range in one direction.
Microwave the lemon for 15 seconds before squeezing. Heat breaks down membranes and releases twice the juice.

entries.title: Here's a life hack that actually works: Keep chip bags closed
entries.hook: Here's a life hack that actually works:
explanations: Fold the top of the bag down in triangles then flip it over. The tension holds it shut without a clip.
Wrap a wet paper towel around the bottle and put it in the freezer. Evaporative cooling works in 15 minutes.
Wrap duct tape around the lid in opposite directions leaving two tails to pull. The leverage opens any jar easily.
Dip a toothpick in wood glue and hammer it into the loose joint. Snap off the excess. The table becomes rock solid.

entries.title: You've been doing this wrong your whole life: Untie knots with a fork

explanations: Stick a fork into the knot and twist. The tines separate the strands and the knot loosens instantly.
Wrap a spring from an old pen around the base of the charging cable. It prevents fraying at the stress point.
Heat the sticker with a hairdryer for 30 seconds. The adhesive softens and it peels off without residue.
Dip a toothpick in wood glue and hammer it into the loose joint. Snap off the excess. The table becomes rock solid.

entries.title: This simple trick changes everything: Keep bananas fresh longer

explanations: Wrap the stem of the banana bunch in plastic wrap. It traps the ethylene gas and slows ripening by days.

Microwave the lemon for 15 seconds before squeezing. Heat breaks down membranes and releases twice the juice.

Place a curved piece of aluminum foil behind your router. It reflects the signal forward and can double range in one direction.

entries.title: This life hack will save you time: Peel garlic in 10 seconds
entries.hook: This life hack will save you time:
explanations: Put garlic cloves in a metal bowl cover with another bowl and shake hard for 10 seconds. The skin falls right off.
Fold the top of the bag down in triangles then flip it over. The tension holds it shut without a clip.
Wrap duct tape around the lid in opposite directions leaving two tails to pull. The leverage opens any jar easily.
Heat the sticker with a hairdryer for 30 seconds. The adhesive softens and it peels off without residue.

explanations: Safety pin socks together before washing. They stay paired through the entire laundry cycle.

Rub pencil graphite along the teeth of a stuck zipper. The graphite acts as dry lubricant and it glides smoothly.

hacks: Find wall studs with a magnet
explanations: Stick a fork into the knot and twist. The tines separate the strands and the knot loosens instantly.
Run a magnet along the wall until it sticks to a screw or nail. That's where the stud is.

Heat the sticker with a hairdryer for 30 seconds. The adhesive softens and it peels off without residue.

entries.title: This simple trick changes everything: Find wall studs with a magnet
entries.hook: This simple trick changes everything:
hacks: Find wall studs with a magnet
explanations: Run a magnet along the wall until it sticks to a screw or nail. That's where the stud is.
Wrap a spring from an old pen around the base of the charging cable. It prevents fraying at the stress point.
Fold the top of the bag down in triangles then flip it over. The tension holds it shut without a clip.
Heat the sticker with a hairdryer for 30 seconds. The adhesive softens and it peels off without residue.

entries.title: You've been doing this wrong your whole life: Remove a stripped screw
entries.hook: You've been doing this wrong your whole life:
explanations: Place a rubber band between the screwdriver and the screw head. The extra grip lets you turn it out.
Heat the sticker with a hairdryer for 30 seconds. The adhesive softens and it peels off without residue.
Dip a toothpick in wood glue and hammer it into the loose joint. Snap off the excess. The table becomes rock solid.
Microwave the lemon for 15 seconds before squeezing. Heat breaks down membranes and releases twice the juice.

entries.title: Here's a life hack that actually works: Never lose a sock again
entries.hook: Here's a life hack that actually works:
explanations: Safety pin socks together before washing. They stay paired through the entire laundry cycle.
Wrap a spring from an old pen around the base of the charging cable. It prevents fraying at the stress point.
Place a rubber band between the screwdriver and the screw head. The extra grip lets you turn it out.
Draw over the stain with a dry erase marker then wipe both off together. The solvents lift the permanent ink.

entries.title: This simple trick changes everything: Peel garlic in 10 seconds
entries.hook: This simple trick changes everything:
explanations: Put garlic cloves in a metal bowl cover with another bowl and shake hard for 10 seconds. The skin falls right off.
Fold the top of the bag down in triangles then flip it over. The tension holds it shut without a clip.
Store wireless earbuds in the case upside down. Contacts don't connect so trickle charging stops and battery lasts longer.
Fold cables in thirds and loop a twist tie around the middle. They stay coiled and never tangle in your bag.

entries.title: This life hack will save you time: Cool drinks faster
entries.hook: This life hack will save you time:
explanations: Wrap a wet paper towel around the bottle and put it in the freezer. Evaporative cooling works in 15 minutes.
Wrap duct tape around the lid in opposite directions leaving two tails to pull. The leverage opens any jar easily.
Store ice cream in a ziplock bag inside the carton. The bag prevents ice crystals from forming so it stays scoopable.
Microwave the lemon for 15 seconds before squeezing. Heat breaks down membranes and releases twice the juice.

entries.title: You've been doing this wrong your whole life: Remove permanent marker
entries.hook: You've been doing this wrong your whole life:
explanations: Draw over the stain with a dry erase marker then wipe both off together. The solvents lift the permanent ink.
Wrap duct tape around the lid in opposite directions leaving two tails to pull. The leverage opens any jar easily.
Store wireless earbuds in the case upside down. Contacts don't connect so trickle charging stops and battery lasts longer.
Microwave the lemon for 15 seconds before squeezing. Heat breaks down membranes and releases twice the juice.
